name: Sourdough Rye Starter
recipeInstructions:
In a glass or ceramic bowl, or a jar that has been scalded, combine the rye flour and yeast. Add the lukewarm water and mix well until there are no dry spots. If using an onion slice, add it now; cover with plastic wrap and pierce the surface with a fork to release gases. Place the container in a warm, draft-free location where the temperature remains at an even 85°F for three days. Stir several times each day.
After three days, remove the onion slice (if used) and refrigerate the starter until ready to use. The starter with the onion imparts a strong flavor and should only be used with compatible rye recipes. A rye starter without the onion can be used in any rye sourdough recipe.
To replenish the starter, discard all but 1 cup of the existing starter. Transfer this amount back into the jar or container. Add 2 cups of fresh rye flour and 1 tablespoon of yeast. Mix well until smooth. Allow the mixture to rest at room temperature for 12 hours. If the starter does not appear bubbly after this period, sprinkle 1 tablespoon of yeast over it and stir thoroughly. As with whole wheat starters, expect longer rising times when using dough made with this rye starter.
